Final go/no-go delivery baseline review after frontend non-root fix. Verify all images non-root, Compose/proxy/migration/health, CI/secrets/docs. Do not edit. Reply APPROVED or Critical/High only.

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: attested
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

Required evidence: review-findings, residual-risks

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
`criteriaSatisfied[].status` must be exactly one of: satisfied, not-satisfied, not-applicable.
`commandsRun[].result` must be exactly one of: passed, failed, not-run.
`manualNotes` and `notes` are optional strings; an empty string means no note and does not satisfy `manual-notes` evidence.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

'; dotnet --version 2>&1; printf '

--- backend/Dockerfile
     1	FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:9.0 AS base
     2	ARG APP_UID=10001
     3	ARG APP_GID=10001
     4	RUN groupadd --system --gid ${APP_GID} inventoryflow \
     5	    && useradd --uid ${APP_UID} --gid inventoryflow --create-home --home-dir /home/inventoryflow --shell /usr/sbin/nologin inventoryflow \
     6	    && mkdir -p /src /app /home/inventoryflow/.nuget/packages \
     7	    && chown -R inventoryflow:inventoryflow /src /app /home/inventoryflow
     8	ENV DOTNET_CLI_HOME=/home/inventoryflow \
     9	    NUGET_PACKAGES=/home/inventoryflow/.nuget/packages
    10	WORKDIR /src
    11	USER inventoryflow
    12	
    13	FROM base AS restore
    14	COPY --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow Directory.Build.props Directory.Packages.props InventoryFlow.sln ./
    15	COPY --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow src/InventoryFlow.Api/InventoryFlow.Api.csproj src/InventoryFlow.Api/
    16	COPY --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow src/InventoryFlow.Application/InventoryFlow.Application.csproj src/InventoryFlow.Application/
    17	COPY --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow src/InventoryFlow.Domain/InventoryFlow.Domain.csproj src/InventoryFlow.Domain/
    18	COPY --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure.csproj src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/
    19	COPY --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow src/InventoryFlow.Shared/InventoryFlow.Shared.csproj src/InventoryFlow.Shared/
    20	RUN dotnet restore src/InventoryFlow.Api/InventoryFlow.Api.csproj
    21	
    22	FROM restore AS build
    23	COPY --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow src ./src
    24	RUN dotnet build src/InventoryFlow.Api/InventoryFlow.Api.csproj --configuration Release --no-restore
    25	
    26	FROM build AS publish
    27	RUN dotnet publish src/InventoryFlow.Api/InventoryFlow.Api.csproj --configuration Release --no-build --output /app/publish
    28	
    29	FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:9.0 AS final
    30	ARG APP_UID=10001
    31	ARG APP_GID=10001
    32	RUN groupadd --system --gid ${APP_GID} inventoryflow \
    33	    && useradd --uid ${APP_UID} --gid inventoryflow --create-home --home-dir /home/inventoryflow --shell /usr/sbin/nologin inventoryflow \
    34	    && mkdir -p /app \
    35	    && chown inventoryflow:inventoryflow /app
    36	WORKDIR /app
    37	EXPOSE 8080
    38	ENV ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://+:8080
    39	COPY --from=publish --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow /app/publish .
    40	USER inventoryflow
    41	ENTRYPOINT ["dotnet", "InventoryFlow.Api.dll"]
    42	
    43	FROM build AS migrator
    44	COPY --chown=inventoryflow:inventoryflow .config .config
    45	RUN dotnet tool restore
    46	ENTRYPOINT ["dotnet", "ef", "database", "update", "--project", "src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure", "--startup-project", "src/InventoryFlow.Api"]
--- frontend/Dockerfile
     1	FROM oven/bun:1.3.14 AS build
     2	WORKDIR /app
     3	
     4	COPY package.json bun.lock ./
     5	RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
     6	COPY . .
     7	ARG VITE_API_BASE_URL=/
     8	ENV VITE_API_BASE_URL=$VITE_API_BASE_URL
     9	RUN bun run build
    10	
    11	FROM nginxinc/nginx-unprivileged:1.27-alpine AS final
    12	COPY --chown=nginx:nginx nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    13	COPY --chown=nginx:nginx --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
    14	EXPOSE 8080
--- docker-compose.yml
     1	services:
     2	  sql:
     3	    image: mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest
     4	    environment:
     5	      ACCEPT_EULA: "Y"
     6	      MSSQL_PID: Developer
     7	      MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD: ${MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD:?Set MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD in .env}
     8	    ports:
     9	      - "1433:1433"
    10	    volumes:
    11	      - sql-data:/var/opt/mssql
    12	    healthcheck:
    13	      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "/opt/mssql-tools18/bin/sqlcmd -S localhost -U sa -P \"$$MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD\" -C -Q \"SELECT 1\""]

--- README.md
     1	# Inventory Flow
     2	
     3	**Inventory Flow** is an enterprise-oriented inventory management SaaS for tracking products, stock, warehouses, suppliers, purchasing, sales, and operational activity from one workspace.
     4	
     5	> [!NOTE]
     6	> The project is under active development. The current milestone establishes the production foundations: Clean Architecture, Identity persistence, refresh-token lifecycle rules, a responsive React shell, and a dashboard experience.
     7	
     8	## Highlights
     9	
    10	- **Clean .NET backend** — ASP.NET Core 9, MediatR, FluentValidation, Serilog, centralized package management, RFC 7807 errors, rate limiting, health checks, and xUnit coverage.
    11	- **Identity-ready persistence** — SQL Server, ASP.NET Core Identity, GUID keys, refresh-token storage, EF Core migrations, and retry-aware database configuration.
    12	- **Enterprise React UI** — React 19, TypeScript, Vite, shadcn/ui, Hugeicons, Tailwind CSS v4, responsive navigation, light/dark themes, and a code-split dashboard.
    13	- **Professional workflow** — public repository, `main`/`develop` branches, scoped feature branches, pull requests, reviews, and automated local validation commands.
    14	
    15	## Architecture
    16	
    17	```text
    18	React SPA (frontend)
    19	        │ HTTPS / REST
    20	ASP.NET Core API (backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api)
    21	        │
    22	Application → Domain ← Infrastructure
    23	        │                  │
    24	     MediatR            EF Core / Identity
    25	                           │
    26	                       SQL Server
    27	```
    28	
    29	```text
    30	backend/
    31	  src/
    32	    InventoryFlow.Api/             HTTP pipeline and API composition
    33	    InventoryFlow.Application/     use cases, handlers, validators, mappings
    34	    InventoryFlow.Domain/          entities, value objects, business rules
    35	    InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/  EF Core, Identity, persistence integrations
    36	    InventoryFlow.Shared/          cross-cutting contracts
    37	  tests/
    38	    InventoryFlow.UnitTests/
    39	    InventoryFlow.IntegrationTests/
    40	
    41	frontend/
    42	  src/
    43	    app/                           configuration, providers, routing
    44	    components/                    shadcn primitives and shared UI
    45	    features/                      feature-owned pages, hooks, schemas, APIs
    46	    layouts/                       route layouts
    47	    lib/                           shared client infrastructure
    48	    store/                         Zustand client-only state
    49	```
    50	
    51	## Technology
    52	
    53	| Area | Tools |
    54	| --- | --- |
    55	| Backend | ASP.NET Core 9, C#, EF Core, SQL Server, ASP.NET Identity, MediatR, FluentValidation, AutoMapper, Serilog |
    56	| Frontend | React 19, TypeScript, Vite, React Router v7, TanStack Query/Table, React Hook Form, Zod, Axios, Zustand |
    57	| Design | shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, Hugeicons, Recharts, Sonner, Motion |
    58	| Tooling | Bun, xUnit, EF Core local tool manifest |
    59	
    60	## Get started
    61	
    62	### Prerequisites
    63	
    64	- Docker with Docker Compose
    65	- Or, for running services directly: [.NET SDK 9.0.316](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/9.0), [Bun 1.3+](https://bun.sh/), and SQL Server 2022+
    66	
    67	### Docker Compose (recommended)
    68	
    69	```bash
    70	cp .env.example .env
    71	# Edit .env: provide a strong SQL Server SA password and a random JWT signing key (at least 32 bytes).
    72	docker compose up --build
    73	```
    74	
    75	Compose is a local-development stack only. SQL Server must become healthy before its one-shot `migrate` service applies EF migrations; the API and web services then start. Open `http://localhost:8080`; the SPA proxies `/api` to the API and uses SPA fallback routing. The API runs in `Development` so refresh cookies remain valid over local HTTP. Stop it with `docker compose down` (add `-v` to remove local database data).
    76	
    77	No credentials or JWT signing keys are committed. Do not use this HTTP Compose configuration for production.
    78	
    79	### Run services directly
    80	
    81	Set configuration outside tracked files, then apply migrations:
    82	
    83	```bash
    84	export ConnectionStrings__InventoryFlowDatabase='Server=localhost,1433;Database=InventoryFlow;User Id=sa;Password=<strong-password>;TrustServerCertificate=True'
    85	export Jwt__SigningKey='<at-least-32-random-bytes>'
    86	cd backend
    87	dotnet tool restore
    88	dotnet restore
    89	dotnet ef database update --project src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure --startup-project src/InventoryFlow.Api
    90	```
    91	
    92	In separate terminals, run `dotnet run --project backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api` and `cd frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run dev`. The Vite server proxies `/api` to `http://localhost:5255`; its frontend is at `http://localhost:5173`.
    93	
    94	## Quality checks
    95	
    96	```bash
    97	# Backend
    98	dotnet build backend/InventoryFlow.sln
    99	dotnet test backend/InventoryFlow.sln
   100	dotnet format backend/InventoryFlow.sln --verify-no-changes --no-restore
   101	
   102	# Frontend
   103	cd frontend
   104	bun run typecheck
   105	bun run lint
   106	bun run build
   107	bunx prettier --check .
   108	```
   109	
   110	## Delivery workflow
   111	
   112	- `main` contains production-ready releases.
   113	- `develop` is the integration branch.
   114	- Frontend work uses `feature/frontend/<feature-name>`.
   115	- Backend work uses `feature/backend/<feature-name>`.
   116	- Fixes use `bugfix/frontend/<description>` or `bugfix/backend/<description>`.
   117	
   118	Every change is committed on a scoped branch, validated locally, reviewed in a pull request into `develop`, and promoted to `main` for release. GitHub Actions validates pull requests and `main` with backend restore/build/test/format and frontend frozen Bun install/typecheck/lint/build.
   119	
   120	## Current scope
   121	
   122	Implemented foundations include the API pipeline, global problem-details handling, health checks, SQL Server Identity schema, refresh-token domain model and migration, and the responsive dashboard shell.
   123	
   124	Upcoming vertical slices cover role and permission enforcement, Redis caching, and Hangfire jobs. This repository intentionally contains no CD, registry publishing, production TLS, production secrets, or API startup migrations.
--- frontend/README.md

     2

     4

--- README.md
     1	# Inventory Flow
     2	
     3	**Inventory Flow** is an enterprise-oriented inventory management SaaS for tracking products, stock, warehouses, suppliers, purchasing, sales, and operational activity from one workspace.
     4	
     5	> [!NOTE]
     6	> The project is under active development. The current milestone establishes the production foundations: Clean Architecture, Identity persistence, refresh-token lifecycle rules, a responsive React shell, and a dashboard experience.
     7	
     8	## Highlights
     9	
    10	- **Clean .NET backend** — ASP.NET Core 9, MediatR, FluentValidation, Serilog, centralized package management, RFC 7807 errors, rate limiting, health checks, and xUnit coverage.
    11	- **Identity-ready persistence** — SQL Server, ASP.NET Core Identity, GUID keys, refresh-token storage, EF Core migrations, and retry-aware database configuration.
    12	- **Enterprise React UI** — React 19, TypeScript, Vite, shadcn/ui, Hugeicons, Tailwind CSS v4, responsive navigation, light/dark themes, and a code-split dashboard.
    13	- **Professional workflow** — public repository, `main`/`develop` branches, scoped feature branches, pull requests, reviews, and automated local validation commands.
    14	
    15	## Architecture
    16	
    17	```text
    18	React SPA (frontend)
    19	        │ HTTPS / REST
    20	ASP.NET Core API (backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api)
    21	        │
    22	Application → Domain ← Infrastructure
    23	        │                  │
    24	     MediatR            EF Core / Identity
    25	                           │
    26	                       SQL Server
    27	```
    28	
    29	```text
    30	backend/
    31	  src/
    32	    InventoryFlow.Api/             HTTP pipeline and API composition
    33	    InventoryFlow.Application/     use cases, handlers, validators, mappings
    34	    InventoryFlow.Domain/          entities, value objects, business rules
    35	    InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/  EF Core, Identity, persistence integrations
    36	    InventoryFlow.Shared/          cross-cutting contracts
    37	  tests/
    38	    InventoryFlow.UnitTests/
    39	    InventoryFlow.IntegrationTests/
    40	
    41	frontend/
    42	  src/
    43	    app/                           configuration, providers, routing
    44	    components/                    shadcn primitives and shared UI
    45	    features/                      feature-owned pages, hooks, schemas, APIs
    46	    layouts/                       route layouts
    47	    lib/                           shared client infrastructure
    48	    store/                         Zustand client-only state
    49	```
    50	
    51	## Technology
    52	
    53	| Area | Tools |
    54	| --- | --- |
    55	| Backend | ASP.NET Core 9, C#, EF Core, SQL Server, ASP.NET Identity, MediatR, FluentValidation, AutoMapper, Serilog |
    56	| Frontend | React 19, TypeScript, Vite, React Router v7, TanStack Query/Table, React Hook Form, Zod, Axios, Zustand |
    57	| Design | shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, Hugeicons, Recharts, Sonner, Motion |
    58	| Tooling | Bun, xUnit, EF Core local tool manifest |
    59	
    60	## Get started
    61	
    62	### Prerequisites
    63	
    64	- Docker with Docker Compose
    65	- Or, for running services directly: [.NET SDK 9.0.316](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/9.0), [Bun 1.3+](https://bun.sh/), and SQL Server 2022+
    66	
    67	### Docker Compose (recommended)
    68	
    69	```bash
    70	cp .env.example .env
    71	# Edit .env: provide a strong SQL Server SA password and a random JWT signing key (at least 32 bytes).
    72	docker compose up --build
    73	```
    74	
    75	Compose is a local-development stack only. SQL Server must become healthy before its one-shot `migrate` service applies EF migrations; the API and web services then start. Open `http://localhost:8080`; the SPA proxies `/api` to the API and uses SPA fallback routing. The API runs in `Development` so refresh cookies remain valid over local HTTP. Stop it with `docker compose down` (add `-v` to remove local database data).
    76	
    77	No credentials or JWT signing keys are committed. Do not use this HTTP Compose configuration for production.
    78	
    79	### Run services directly
    80	
    81	Set configuration outside tracked files, then apply migrations:
    82	
    83	```bash
    84	export ConnectionStrings__InventoryFlowDatabase='Server=localhost,1433;Database=InventoryFlow;User Id=sa;Password=<strong-password>;TrustServerCertificate=True'
    85	export Jwt__SigningKey='<at-least-32-random-bytes>'
    86	cd backend
    87	dotnet tool restore
    88	dotnet restore
    89	dotnet ef database update --project src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure --startup-project src/InventoryFlow.Api
    90	```
    91	
    92	In separate terminals, run `dotnet run --project backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api` and `cd frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run dev`. The Vite server proxies `/api` to `http://localhost:5255`; its frontend is at `http://localhost:5173`.
    93	
    94	## Quality checks
    95	
    96	```bash
    97	# Backend
    98	dotnet build backend/InventoryFlow.sln
    99	dotnet test backend/InventoryFlow.sln
   100	dotnet format backend/InventoryFlow.sln --verify-no-changes --no-restore
   101	
   102	# Frontend
   103	cd frontend
   104	bun run typecheck
   105	bun run lint
   106	bun run build
   107	bunx prettier --check .
   108	```
   109	
   110	## Delivery workflow
   111	
   112	- `main` contains production-ready releases.
   113	- `develop` is the integration branch.
   114	- Frontend work uses `feature/frontend/<feature-name>`.
   115	- Backend work uses `feature/backend/<feature-name>`.
   116	- Fixes use `bugfix/frontend/<description>` or `bugfix/backend/<description>`.
   117	
   118	Every change is committed on a scoped branch, validated locally, reviewed in a pull request into `develop`, and promoted to `main` for release. GitHub Actions validates pull requests and `main` with backend restore/build/test/format and frontend frozen Bun install/typecheck/lint/build.
   119	
   120	## Current scope
   121	
   122	Implemented foundations include the API pipeline, global problem-details handling, health checks, SQL Server Identity schema, refresh-token domain model and migration, and the responsive dashboard shell.
   123	
   124	Upcoming vertical slices cover role and permission enforcement, Redis caching, and Hangfire jobs. This repository intentionally contains no CD, registry publishing, production TLS, production secrets, or API startup migrations.
--- frontend/README.md
     1	# Inventory Flow Web
     2	
     3	The React single-page application for Inventory Flow.
     4	
     5	## Stack
     6	
     7	React 19, TypeScript, Vite, shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, React Router v7, TanStack Query/Table, React Hook Form, Zod, Axios, Zustand, Hugeicons, Recharts, Sonner, and Motion.

name: inventory-flow
services:
  api:
    build:
      context: /home/adnan/Projects/Inventory-Flow/backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      target: final
    depends_on:
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
        required: true
    environment:
      ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT: Development
      ConnectionStrings__InventoryFlowDatabase: Server=sql,1433;Database=InventoryFlow;User
        Id=sa;Password=ValidPassword_123!;TrustServerCertificate=True;Encrypt=False
      Jwt__SigningKey: "01234567890123456789012345678901"
    networks:
      default: null
    ports:
      - mode: ingress
        target: 8080
        published: "5255"
        protocol: tcp
  migrate:
    build:
      context: /home/adnan/Projects/Inventory-Flow/backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      target: migrator
    depends_on:
      sql:
        condition: service_healthy
        required: true
    environment:
      ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT: Development
      ConnectionStrings__InventoryFlowDatabase: Server=sql,1433;Database=InventoryFlow;User
        Id=sa;Password=ValidPassword_123!;TrustServerCertificate=True;Encrypt=False
      Jwt__SigningKey: "01234567890123456789012345678901"
    networks:
      default: null
    restart: 'no'
  sql:
    environment:
      ACCEPT_EULA: 'Y'
      MSSQL_PID: Developer
      MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD: ValidPassword_123!
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - /opt/mssql-tools18/bin/sqlcmd -S localhost -U sa -P "$$MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD"
          -C -Q "SELECT 1"
      timeout: 5s
      interval: 10s
      retries: 12
      start_period: 20s
    image: mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest
    networks:
      default: null
    ports:
      - mode: ingress
        target: 1433
        published: "1433"
        protocol: tcp
    volumes:
      - type: volume
        source: sql-data
        target: /var/opt/mssql
        volume: {}
  web:
    build:
      context: /home/adnan/Projects/Inventory-Flow/frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      args:
        VITE_API_BASE_URL: /
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_started
        required: true
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
        required: true
    networks:
      default: null
    ports:
      - mode: ingress
        target: 8080
        published: "8080"
        protocol: tcp
networks:
  default:
    name: inventory-flow_default
volumes:
  sql-data:
    name: inventory-flow_sql-data
